scenarios.name: ChatGPT semantic proposal for governance review
scenarios.expected_result: accepted bounded proposal rendered through continuity cockpit
confusing_labels: continuity status versus semantic proposal validation status
session replay visibility versus durable replay
missing_evidence: no automated browser interaction evidence for operator comprehension

scenarios.name: ChatGPT semantic proposal with unsafe execution wording
confusing_labels: CONTINUITY_BOUNDARY_VIOLATION may sound more severe than proposal rejection
duplicate_concepts: blocked lifecycle entry and continuity boundary violation
scenarios.helped_or_added_noise: helped by rejecting unsafe wording, with some report verbosity

scenarios.name: Valid semantic proposal imported from semantic_proposal.json with hash verification
scenarios.expected_result: accepted bounded proposal with HASH_VERIFIED integrity status
confusing_labels: HASH_VERIFIED could be overread as semantic correctness unless labeled carefully
CERTIFIED_FOR_CONTINUITY_INGESTION could be overread as approval
missing_evidence: browser automation evidence for actual selected file import
scenarios.helped_or_added_noise: helped most of the three scenarios by making the proposal artifact tangible and verifiable

cross_scenario_findings: The cockpit helps operators verify acceptance, rejection, authority boundaries, artifact integrity, and replay/lifecycle visibility.
The cockpit adds noise when an operator needs a compact rejection reason or a single recommended next action.
The file-import plus hash-verification path is the strongest proving baseline for non-copy/paste semantic transport.
The sidepanel should separate execution-capable runtime controls from read-only proving controls before a local bridge is added.
ux_issues: Runtime controls and read-only proving controls share the same control grid.
Authority boundary labels are correct but repetitive.
Replay Timeline, Replay Sessions, and replay artifact inspection are easy to confuse.
Several validation statuses compete for operator attention.
Hash verification needs a short explanation that integrity is not semantic truth, approval, or safety.
risks: Operator may confuse CERTIFIED_FOR_CONTINUITY_INGESTION with approval.
Operator may over-trust HASH_VERIFIED as semantic correctness.
Current replay is session-local and could be mistaken for durable replay.
Current proving lacks browser automation for real file selection.
Cockpit density may reduce adoption if not consolidated before bridge implementation.
recommended_fixes_before_bridge: Add a compact Recommended Operator Path panel for semantic proposal import.
Add a short rejection summary above detailed artifacts.
Visually separate runtime execution controls from read-only proving controls.
Add an explicit top-level marker that replay is session-only and not durable.
Add browser automation or manual evidence for actual file import.
Keep localhost POST and extension background import deferred.
recommended_next_step: Prepare CHATGPT_AIGOL_LOCAL_BRIDGE_V1 implementation review constrained to explicit local file handoff and operator-selected import.
